channel.title: Comments for page "Empire of Dirt (Part 1)"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Empire of Dirt (Part 1)"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just realized that, unlike its sequels, the "o" in "of" in the title isn't capitalized.

That's going to bother me for the rest of time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man…

This went in the most obvious 2blackhearted4u direction possible. I mean, I was thinking "Djoric won't be this obvious" from page 1. [edit: not page one, but from the first mention of Moloch.]

Very well-written though. So I guess we'll see.

The parts in quote boxes didn't work for me, however. Too overwritten, not enough cool (though some of the bits are cool).

EDIT: A big issue I have is that this feels like a contrivance. Mary-Ann, of all people? You can solve this by having her point out in disbelief 'why her of all people', and have the Foundation people give a good (or good-sounding) answer, but that doesn't happen, unless I missed a thing. I'm willing to believe that in real life it wouldn't, but I think it should be included in here regardless. There's no reason not to make Mary-Ann smarter than the average reader.

EDITx2: Upvoting ultimately because this piece had a big enough of an impact to make any reader keep going, despite the issues.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mostly I'm just bitter that you got the Mushishi opening theme stuck in my head on top of everything else.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm. I'll have to think about this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You have an upvote from Soulless.

Congratulations.

EDIT: But only because killing children.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope. He's a savvy low-aristocracy and morally-bankrupt businessman, but he is not actually Dark of MC&D.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is awesome in every conceivable way. Question. Is the slave-broker/thrall-master a certain Dark?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Equal parts absurdity and horror, Lumancer. Equal parts absurdity and horror.

Think about it: Able went completely mad in his ten thousand years of isolation. It can be assumed that Darkblade and Soulberg suffered the same fate, just in different ways. And really, the states in which we know them would reflect the men that they were before they became what they are now: Able was a solemn, melancholic man who chafed under his slavery until nothing but hate remained. Darkblade was an egomaniacal narcissist. Soulberg was an awkward and incapable swordsman who received his position through nepotism and incompetence from several parties. All three of them went batshit insane.

But not yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I raged hard when I noticed Darkblade and Soulberg, and yet that still wasn't enough to turn me off from how incredibly neatly you've fit all of this into place. Linking The Flesh that Hates to Dae and Moloch works far better than I could have imagined- the volume of references and interconnections you throw at us continues to impress. That Marry Ann's daughter just happens to get chosen feels like a stretch, but there's no way around that, and it's clear that you're going somewhere with it, so that's far from a complaint.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gods, this is going to be a mess when Salah finds out.

Upvoted, you bastard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Goddammit, I knew it. You were going to drop something absolutely horrible on us sooner or later, it was just a matter of when!

*sniff* Take this stupid upvote and cram it where the sun does not shine, please.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You can't hurt me in here, Djoric.

:(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're reading too far into that one.

Wonder-Maker is Wondertainment in an older, trickster-god form. The Nothing-In-All is 055. The Eternal Texts are 582. Carthac, Molug, and Gothog can be whoever you want them to be, really, though the first of those is based somewhat off of a acip in the tale Guard Duty. I was struggling for things to fill slots, you see.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is Darkblade the fourth blade because April is the fourth month, or am I reading too much into it?'

Also, am I wrong to think you intended the Wonder-Maker to be a prototypical Dr. Wondertainment? Is the Nothing-in-All a reference to the deleted All in Nothing, or whatever the sphere that likes worship was called? I get some of the other references (King-in-Scarlet, Mother of Them All, the Hanged King), but am a bit lost on some of the others. Would you be willing to elucidate?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Both of those segments take place in the late days of the Daevite Empire, c. 11000 BCE or so. They're part of a story that is running parallel to Mary-Ann and Salah's story in the present day. The particular event described, being the triumphal march of Ab-Leshal (that's Able) where he returned to the capital city of Daevon with fragments of the Broken God (that is, the Metatron or the Voice) and the Tree of Life, was mentioned in The Tick Tock Gospel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Said every Chaos cultist ever. Asides from Khornates, they just go "KHOOORNE!" or "GHAAAA!" for the less intellectually gifted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Any chance of some spoilers? I feel like I'm missing most of the stuff in the start and finish.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Foundation universe, in my eyes, is equal parts horror and absurdity, and half of the horror comes from the absurdity. So yes. I did sneak a SoulBerg reference into this. And Darkblade. Because I needed dudes with swords.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did you really sneak a fucking Soulberg reference into this?

Also, great, much improved, very well done, etc. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Djoric, you horrible bastard.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, those are the ones that seem to get shit done, so I'd just call it pragmatism.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do like the descriptions of her ongoing thoughts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The King Crowned in Shame does not allow the unwashed masses into his service.

For remember, as the Prophet Alan once howled:

"Moloch! Moloch! Nightmare of Moloch! Moloch the loveless! Mental Moloch! Moloch the heavy judger of men! Moloch, whose guts are men and his shit their works!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well done - I sure didn't expect this (before reading the draft in chat , anyhow) and there's a thousand neat details i like. Like, Mary-Ann having enough cultural awareness to compare 610 to the flood, 610 living in Daevite lands …the sacrifice of draft mammoths…
Also , Daevites clearly have a knack for worshipping everything vile and disgusting out there, don't they.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be honest, not quite to my taste. It's a bit too unlikely, a bit too soap-opera tragedy. Also, I'm surprised the Foundation doesn't have anyone my sympathetic and persuasive on retainer to be the ambassador to the 089 family.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am almost in tears, oh my god why

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maniacal laughter intensifies

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You… you motherfucker.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
DANGER ZONE!

Ahm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What have I become,
My sweetest friend?
Everyone I know
Goes away, in the end.

And you could have it all…

It begins.
